The Slice class — Slicing netCDF variables and arrays¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

class slicer.slicer.Slicer(object)¶

Public methods¶

Slicer(dimensions):
Parameters:dimensions (a list of class instances derived from slicing.dimensions.dimensions, the same number as the shape of the array(s) to slice.) – the objects which will handle each of the dimensions of the array(s) to slice
__call__(variable,directive,visitor,verbose=0,**kargs):
Parameters:
  • variable (a numpy array or any object being able to be sliced) – the array to be sliced
  • directive (dictionary) – contains entries with the same name as the dimensions, specifying how to slice the array
  • visitor (object implementing the right methods) – will be called repeatedly as the variable is sliced.
  • verbose (int) – sets the level of output during the operations
  • kargs – any parameters you want to be sent down to the visitor

This method does all the work of calling the dimension objects and slicing along all the dimensions. The visitor should have the following methods (please see for example slicing.slice2array.Slice2Array and slicing.slice2list.Slice2List):

def __call__(self,dimensions,variable,src,dst,meta,is_interval,**kargs):
    # called for each slice to copy from source to destination
    # dimensions: list of string giving the dimension names
    # variable: the array being sliced
    # src: list of slices to use from the variable
    # dst: list of slices to use to the destination array
    # meta: a dictionary representing the metadata taken from the directive (e.g. date=list of dates, levels = list of level) once intervals have been resolved
    # is_interval: an array of booleans stating whether the successive dimensions are
        discrete values or intervals

def preProcess(self,dimensions,variable,shape,slices,metadata):
   # called once right before __call__ is called for possible initialisations
   # dimensions: a list of dimension-derived class intances
   # variable: the array to be sliced
   # shape: the shape of the array to be sliced
   # slices: the list of slices which will be applied
   # metadata: see above

def postProcess(self,dimensions,variable,shape,slices,metadata):
  # called once right before the slicing is done
  # the parameters are identical to the preProcess method

Private methods¶

slice(dimensions,variable,slices,metadata,is_interval,visitor,index = 0,src=[],dst=[],meta=[],**kargs):

This method is the recursive method which does all the slicing work. Please see the source for details.
